news.summary: INX Digital Company, Inc. shared its financial results for Q1 2025. The company's focus on expanding crypto offerings and a pending acquisition by Republic valued at up to $60 million marked significant milestones. Financial highlights included total working capital of $11.2 million ($13.7 million adjusted), Q1 trading fees at $52K (down 42% YoY), net operating loss of $4.1 million, and a reserve fund of $34.3 million for customer protection. Despite challenges, INX remains positioned to capitalize on the growing tokenization market and deliver regulated liquidity in the digital asset ecosystem.
news.headline: INX Digital Q1 2025 results: $11.2M total working capital, $52K trading fees, $4.1M net loss. Acquired by Republic for $60M.

news.summary: According to Rob Bredow, the head of Industrial Light & Magic, visual effects companies should carefully consider their use of AI tools.
news.headline: "ILM releases 'Star Wars: Field Guide' short film using generative AI. Boss emphasizes careful use of AI in VFX."

news.summary: Tether has recently minted $1 billion USDt tokens on Tron, surpassing Ethereum's authorized supply. Tron has $73.7 billion authorized USDT, while Ethereum has $74.5 billion. In terms of circulating supply, Tron leads with $73.6 billion USDT compared to Ethereum's $71.8 billion. Tether mints tokens to replenish their inventory on blockchain networks for future issuance demands. Tron and Ethereum battle for USDT supply dominance, with Tron previously leading but Ethereum taking the lead in 2025 before being surpassed again by Tron. Solana has the third-most USDT supply with $2.3 billion authorized. Tether's circulation hit a record high of $150 million, giving it 61% market share of USD stablecoins. Circle, its competitor, holds $60.4 billion in stablecoins.
news.headline: Tron minted $1B USDT tokens surpassing Ethereum's supply, as Tether replenishes its inventory for future issuance requests. Circulating supply: Tron-$73.6B, Ethereum-$71.8B. Solana ranks third with $2.3B USDT. Avalanche has $1.8B authorized USDT. Tether's total circulation hits $150M. Circle holds $60.4B stablecoins.

news.summary: In response to escalating tariff volatility impacting businesses, executives like Ridhima Kahn from Dapper Labs are advocating for the utilization of blockchain-powered digital markets as an alternative. The shift towards digital collectibles is driven by a desire for enhanced fan engagement, with notable brands like NBA, NFL, and Disney already finding success in this space. Average NFT sales are up by 7% quarter-over-quarter, with NFL All Day and NBA Top Shot generating $2.5 million and $5.6 million respectively. Total value locked is at an all-time high of $44.4 million on Flow, signaling broader blockchain use beyond NFTs. Enhanced user experiences and evolving regulatory support are boosting blockchain adoption. Collaborations with well-known brands like NBA Top Shot on OpenSea are efficient in meeting fan demands. Companies are leveraging blockchain to enhance fan experiences by offering personalized, valuable, and globally accessible digital collectibles. The trend towards digital goods is not replacing physical merchandise but expanding revenue streams and engagement avenues for brands. The future of fan engagement lies in mobile platforms and continued exploration of digital spaces by brands, contingent on offering utility and value to consumers.
news.headline: Tariffs prompt NFL, NBA to prioritize digital merch; Avg. NFT sales rose by 7%, sales $2.5M (NFL) $5.6M (NBA). Blockchain providing utility for popular brands, engaging global fan bases. Regulatory support boosts industry confidence. Mobile fandom seen as next big trend.

news.summary: A public opinion poll commissioned by CUPE Ontario in Toronto ahead of the provincial budget aimed to assess Ontarians' views on various topics including taxation, public service funding, health-care privatization, and job creation. The survey focused on evaluating opinions on recent decisions made by the Ford Conservatives. CUPE Ontario President Fred Hahn highlighted that Doug Ford and his party claim to prioritize 'the people' and were re-elected to protect Ontario jobs and workers. The poll gathered insights from a significant number of respondents to determine the prevailing attitudes towards these key issues.
news.headline: Ontarians expect government to invest in public services to defend jobs. CUPE poll gauged attitudes on taxation, funding, privatization, job creation. Fred Hahn calls on Ford Conservatives to fulfill promises.

news.summary: Alibaba Group Holding (NYSE: BABA) released its fiscal fourth-quarter results showing a 7% annual revenue increase to $32.58 billion, falling short of the expected $33.08 billion. The adjusted earnings per ADS were $1.73, surpassing analyst estimates of $1.48, with adjusted net income rising by 22% to $4.11 billion. Revenue from Taobao and Tmall Group rose by 9% to $13.97 billion, while Alibaba International Digital Commerce Group revenue increased by 22% to $4.63 billion due to cross-border business growth. Local Services Group revenue grew by 10% to $2.22 billion, driven by Amap and Ele.me order growth. US Listed Chinese Stocks also surged as import tariffs were eased between nations. For the detailed article, refer to Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Alibaba stock drops 5% after missing revenue estimates, reports $32.58B revenue (7% YoY growth) and $4.11B net income (22% YoY growth).

news.summary: In New York, Datavant, a prominent health data platform company, revealed the acquisition of Aetion, a top-tier real-world evidence (RWE) platform utilized for vital decision-making in healthcare. The agreement solidifies the merging of Aetion with Datavant to furnish a comprehensive RWE platform. This collaboration aims to streamline the research process for healthcare and life sciences organizations, facilitating quicker answers to inquiries regarding the clinical effects of therapies under development or already in circulation. This move signifies a strategic step towards enhancing data-driven insights within the healthcare sector.
news.headline: Datavant acquires Aetion to empower healthcare organizations with high-quality, scalable real-world evidence. #healthdata #RWE #acquisition.

news.summary: AMSTERDAM--Prosus N.V. (Prosus) and Despegar, Latin America’s top online travel agency, have officially finalized Prosus’s acquisition of Despegar (NYSE: DESP) for $19.50 per share. This purchase price was agreed upon following approval from Despegar's board of directors and shareholders, as well as obtaining all necessary regulatory approvals. The completion of this transaction signifies a crucial step in Prosus’s plan to broaden and fortify its presence in the market.
news.headline: Prosus acquires Despegar for $19.50 per share, marking a milestone in expansion strategy.

news.summary: A real estate market leader, CoStar Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: CSGP), based in Arlington, Virginia, announced its recognition of Homes.com, Matterport, and VHT Studios by Matterport by T3 Sixty in the annual 2025 Tech 200 list. This list acknowledges the top technology products for brokerages, teams, and agents in the industry. The 2025 Tech 200 list showcases 200 products specializing in online real estate marketplaces, information, analytics, and 3D digital twin technology.
news.headline: CoStar Group's Homes.com, Matterport, and VHT Studios recognized by T3 Sixty in 2025 Tech 200 list. Brokerages, teams, agents celebrated with 200 selected products.

news.summary: A company based in Arlington, CoStar Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: CSGP), known for its online real estate services, revealed that their Founder and CEO, Andy Florance, has secured a spot on Commercial Observer’s Power 100 list for the third year running. This list, released yearly, acknowledges the top figures, companies, and groups impacting the future of the industry. Andy Florance's consecutive presence on this list attests to his significant influence.
news.headline: CoStar Group CEO Andy Florance named to Commercial Observer's Power 100 List for third year. (#CSGP)

news.summary: Sharrow Engineering, creator of the innovative Sharrow™ Propeller, revealed plans to move its global headquarters to Michigan Central in downtown Detroit. This development signifies a significant step in the company's advancement and involves the establishment of Sharrow Labs, a new subsidiary focused on developing cutting-edge technologies in propulsion, clean energy, and advanced mobility. Positioned at Newlab in Michigan Central, Sharrow Labs will function as the research hub.
news.headline: Sharrow Engineering moves HQ to Detroit, launches Sharrow Labs for pioneering propulsion, energy, and mobility tech at Michigan Central.

news.summary: The global bottle blow molding machine market, valued at $4.1 billion in 2024, is projected to reach $6.5 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 5.5%. Growth is driven by demand for lightweight and sustainable packaging in industries like beverages and pharmaceuticals. Market challenges include raw material price fluctuations and plastic use regulations. Segmentation by product type: Automatic (55%), Semi-Automatic (25%), Manual (20%). By technology: Extrusion Blow Molding (40%), Injection Blow Molding (30%), Stretch Blow Molding (30%). By material: PET (60%), PP (20%), PE (15%), Others (5%). By end-user industry: Food and Beverage (50%), Pharmaceuticals (15%), Personal Care & Cosmetics (15%), Household Products & Others (20%). By distribution channel: Direct Sales (50%), Distributors (30%), Online Sales (20%). Asia-Pacific leads with 45% revenue share, followed by North America (25%) and Europe (20%). Key drivers include demand for sustainable packaging and smart manufacturing integration. Restraints include fluctuating raw material costs and regulatory pressures. Opportunities lie in bioplastics, recycled materials, and AI-driven manufacturing. Key competitors include Sidel, Krones AG, Aoki Technical Laboratory, HUSKY Injection Molding Systems Ltd.
news.headline: Global bottle blow molding machine market set to reach $6.5 billion by 2034 with a 5.5% CAGR. Asia-Pacific leads with 45% revenue share, North America at 25%, and Europe at 20%. Key drivers: demand for sustainable packaging, automation, and smart technologies. Market challenges: fluctuating raw material costs, plastic usage regulations, and supply chain vulnerabilities. Major competitors: Sidel, Krones AG, Aoki Technical Laboratory, Inc., HUSKY Injection Molding Systems Ltd.

news.summary: The article discusses regenerative agriculture, a farming approach that aims to reverse climate change by improving soil health and biodiversity. This method, which includes practices like minimal soil disturbance, crop diversity, and integration of livestock, is gaining traction globally. The regenerative agriculture market was valued at $8.7 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach $16.8 billion by 2027, growing at a 14.0% CAGR. Factors driving this growth include consumer demand for sustainable food, corporate commitments to sustainability, and government incentives for carbon sequestration. North America leads the market, particularly the United States, due to consumer awareness, supportive policies, and investment in sustainable farming technologies. Europe also shows significant interest in regenerative agriculture.
news.headline: Regenerative agriculture market booming, valued at USD 8.7B in 2022, projected to reach USD 16.8B by 2027, growing at 14% CAGR.

news.summary: In New York, Eko, a technology company known for revolutionizing online product pages with interactive galleries, disclosed plans to establish a cutting-edge "Capture Factory" spanning 70,000 square feet in Bentonville, Arkansas. This expansion aims to facilitate the mass production of Eko's primary product while creating job opportunities in Northwest Arkansas, with a projected addition of 200 new positions by the year's end. The initiative underscores Eko's dedication to enhancing the online presentation of products effectively.
news.headline: Eko to open 70,000 sq ft "Capture Factory" in Bentonville, Arkansas, create 200 jobs by year end, revolutionize online commerce.

news.summary: Bitcoin Season 2 is all about exploring the potential of Bitcoin beyond just being money, focusing on financial use cases including trustless lending against Bitcoin without third-party intermediaries. Discreet Log Contracts (DLCs) allow users to borrow against their Bitcoin by locking it as collateral with smart contract logic ensuring repayment. Trustless bridging could open up new possibilities for Bitcoin to interact with external computation platforms without compromising its decentralized nature. Bitcoin-native DeFi could offer real-world financial access to those excluded from traditional banking, with small changes in code unlocking permissionless finance for all. This evolution in Bitcoin's functionality could revolutionize the asset's utility beyond its traditional store of value role. Listen to the full discussion on The Clear Crypto Podcast for more insights.
news.headline: Bitcoin Season 2 focuses on utility beyond price, exploring trustless lending and bridging function with DeFi tools. Exciting future potential discussed on The Clear Crypto Podcast. Find more episodes on Cointelegraph's Podcasts page.

news.summary: Datasea Inc. (DTSS), a Nevada-based tech company focusing on 5G+AI solutions and acoustic high-tech innovations, revealed its financial stats for the third fiscal quarter ending March 31, 2025. The company disclosed revenue figures of $10.4 million for the quarter, up by 653.6% from $1.38 million in the same period last year. Total revenue for the first nine months of the fiscal year hit $51.9 million, a 164.8% increase from $19.6 million in the prior year. The CEO cited the strong growth of their 5G+AI digital segment in China and successful expansion into industries like finance and healthcare as key revenue drivers. The company also saw improvements in gross profit, net loss reduction, and cash balance growth reaching $0.9 million. Datasea expects a revenue boost to $90 million for fiscal year 2025, indicating a 275% surge from 2024, driven by long-term contracts in 5G+AI and acoustic high-tech segments. Their acoustic high-tech products gained traction with the introduction of new offerings and strategic placements in health and beauty stores. Datasea aims to push profitability by expanding their high-margin acoustic products, pursuing international markets, and growing through partnerships and acquisitions. For more information on Datasea Inc., visit www.dataseainc.com.
news.headline: Datasea reports Q3 2025 revenue of $10.4M, up 653.6% YoY. Expecting full year 2025 revenue of $90M, up 275% from 2024.

news.summary: Applied Materials, Inc. will be announcing its second-quarter financial results on May 15 after the market closes. Analysts predict earnings per share of $2.31, a rise from $2.09 per share in the same period last year. The company anticipates quarterly revenue of $7.12 billion, up from $6.65 billion in the previous year. Analyst Tom O'Malley from Barclays maintained an Equal-Weight rating on the stock but reduced the price target from $190 to $160 on April 22. The current annual dividend yield for Applied Materials is 1.06%, with a quarterly dividend of 46 cents per share ($1.84 annually). Investors are showing interest in potential returns from the company's dividends amidst the recent market attention towards Applied Materials.
news.headline: Applied Materials, Inc. is expected to report Q2 earnings of $2.31/share & revenue of $7.12 billion, with an annual dividend yield of 1.06%.

news.summary: In a recent announcement from Denver, the Consolidated Financial Results section reported that in the first quarter of 2025, core operating expenses totaled $18.0 million. This amount comprises selling, general & administrative expenses (SG&A) of $14.1 million and operations & maintenance expenses (O&M) of $3.9 million. These figures reflect an increase from the first quarter of 2024, where core operating expenses were $16.6 million, with SG&A at $13.5 million and O&M at $3.1 million.
news.headline: Spruce Power Q1 2025: Core operating expenses at $18.0 million, up from $16.6 million in Q1 2024.

news.summary: In Sofia, Bulgaria, Westinghouse Electric Company recently signed memorandums of understanding (MoUs) with seven Bulgarian suppliers to aid in the AP1000 project at the Kozloduy Nuclear Power Plant. The agreements were a result of the Westinghouse Bulgaria Supplier Symposium, where regional suppliers explored opportunities to contribute to AP1000 projects internationally. The MoUs outline opportunities for these suppliers to provide various services for the project.
news.headline: Westinghouse signs MoUs with 7 Bulgarian suppliers to support AP1000 project at Kozloduy NPP, aiming to expand supply chain.

news.summary: The global medical biopreservation equipment market was valued at $3.8 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach around $6.2 billion by 2034, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.0% from 2025 to 2034. This market involves technologies like ultra-low temperature freezers and cryopreservation systems and plays a crucial role in preserving biological materials for medical purposes. Factors driving this growth include increasing chronic diseases, aging populations, and advancements in regenerative medicine. Cryopreservation, which involves storing samples at ultra-low temperatures, is a key growth driver, particularly in fields like fertility preservation and regenerative medicine. The market has applications in pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, and clinical research. North America leads the market with 40% market share, followed by Europe with 30% and Asia Pacific as the fastest-growing region at 20%. The top equipment types include Bio-Freezers, Bio-Refrigerators, and Liquid Nitrogen Tanks. The main applications are in biobanking, drug discovery, and regenerative medicine. Growth drivers include organ transplant demand, chronic diseases, and research funding, while restraints include high costs and regulatory challenges. Key opportunities lie in stem cell and gene therapy advancements.
news.headline: Global medical biopreservation equipment market valued at $3.8 billion in 2024, projected to reach $6.2 billion by 2034 with a 5.0% CAGR. Key drivers include increasing chronic disease prevalence and aging global population. Key segment is cryopreservation. North America leads market with 40% share. Top application is biobanking. Restraints include high initial costs and regulatory challenges. Opportunities in stem cell and gene therapies.

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said Iran would not "bow to any bully" in response to U.S. President Donald Trump's criticism of Tehran during his Gulf tour. "He (Trump) thinks he can come here, chant slogans, and scare us. For us, martyrdom is far sweeter than dying in bed.

news.summary: A partnership has been formed between Saudi Arabian Mining Company (Ma'aden) and MP Materials (NYSE: MP) to establish a comprehensive rare earth supply chain in Saudi Arabia. This collaboration, inked during the U.S.-Saudi Investment Forum 2025 in Riyadh, showcases the commitment from both nations to secure critical mineral supply chains. The agreement entails Ma'aden and MP Materials cooperating on various aspects of the rare earth value chain, including mining, separation, refining, and magnet production. Rare earth magnets are crucial components in industries such as electric vehicles, wind turbines, aerospace systems, and robotics, with a significant projected growth in the coming decade. Ma'aden, with operations in 17 mines and mineral exports to 55 countries in 2024, is ramping up its mining activities in line with Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 economic blueprint. The aim of this collaboration is to establish a global hub for an integrated rare earth value chain.
news.headline: US and Saudi Arabia partner to develop rare earth supply chain, with Ma'aden and MP Materials aiming for global hub.

news.summary: Dublin - A new report on the Vision Inspection Systems Market forecasts a valuation of $9.29 billion by 2032, with a projected growth rate of 7.2% during the period 2025-2032. The report, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, covers offerings such as hardware, software, and services, as well as applications like defect detection. It categorizes the systems into types including PC-based and compact systems, with various inspection modes catered to different end-user industries such as F&B, healthcare, and automotive sectors.
news.headline: Vision Inspection Systems Market to reach $9.29 billion by 2032. AI Integration a key trend in Electronics & Semiconductors.

news.summary: London and Houston-based investment firm Stonepeak, along with Energy Equation Partners, has revealed a deal to acquire a majority stake in JET Tankstellen Deutschland GmbH, a major fuel retailer in Germany and Austria, from a Phillips 66 subsidiary. This acquisition places the value of JET at an enterprise level. Stonepeak focuses on infrastructure and real asset investments while EEP specializes in fuel retail ventures. JET's prominence in the market adds significant value to the deal, showcasing the potential for growth and collaboration in the fuel industry. This partnership signifies a strategic move in the investment landscape, with precise numbers regarding the acquisition remaining undisclosed.
news.headline: Stonepeak and EEP to acquire majority stake in JET from Phillips 66, valuing business at enterprise level.

news.summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom recently announced a proposal to halt new enrollments of undocumented immigrants in the state-funded healthcare program to address a $12 billion deficit in the upcoming $322 billion state spending plan for the fiscal year. The freeze, starting in 2026, would prevent low-income adults without legal status from applying for Medi-Cal, California's Medicaid program. Existing enrollees will retain coverage, with changes affecting adults with "unsatisfactory immigration status" from 2027 requiring a $100 monthly premium. Newsom cited high Medicaid spending and economic volatility influenced by federal tariff policies and stock market instability. He highlighted potential revenue losses of $16 billion due to President Trump's economic policies.
news.headline: Gavin Newsom proposes freezing healthcare access for undocumented immigrants in California's $12 billion deficit; existing enrollees remain covered.

news.summary: The global arterial blood filter market was valued at $980 million in 2024 and is projected to grow at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of approximately 6.5% from 2025 to 2034, reaching around $1.8 billion by 2034. Arterial blood filters are specialized medical devices used in healthcare facilities to remove impurities from arterial blood during surgeries like cardiopulmonary bypass and hemodialysis, ensuring blood quality and patient safety. The market growth is driven by factors such as rising cardiovascular disease rates, technological advancements, increasing surgical procedures, and a focus on patient safety. Key drivers include the global increase in cardiovascular diseases, growth in surgical procedures, technological advancements in filter materials, increased healthcare expenditure, emphasis on blood management and patient safety, aging global population, regulatory support, cost-effectiveness, and competitive dynamics. Market restraints include regulatory hurdles, high manufacturing costs, limited awareness and training in some regions, economic and technological pressures, and global health disruptions. These factors contribute to the growth and challenges faced by the arterial blood filter market.
news.headline: Global arterial blood filter market expected to reach $1.8 billion by 2034, with 6.5% CAGR growth. Market value in 2024 was $980 million. Market driven by rising cardiovascular disease rates, technological advancements, and an increased focus on patient safety in surgical environments. Key drivers include increased incidence of cardiovascular diseases, growth in surgical procedures, technological advancements, increased healthcare expenditure, emphasis on blood management and patient safety, aging global population, regulatory support, cost-effectiveness, and competitive dynamics. Market faces challenges including regulatory hurdles, high manufacturing costs, limited awareness and training, economic and technological pressures, and global health disruptions.

news.summary: In Los Angeles, Capstone Green Energy Holdings, Inc. (CGEH) and Capstone Green Energy, LLC. revealed that Vergent Power, Capstone’s distributor for the Upper Midwest, New England, and Eastern Canada, has successfully obtained a new order for the City of Janesville, Wisconsin. The order involves the installation of additional Capstone microturbines to upgrade the city's wastewater treatment facility. The City of Janesville, a loyal client who has been utilizing Capstone microturbines for several years, will be enhancing their operations with this new installation. The exact number of microturbines or the monetary value of the order was not specified in the announcement.
news.headline: City of Janesville invests in sustainability with new Microturbines from Capstone Green Energy; Follow-on order secured for wastewater treatment facility in Wisconsin. #CleanPower

news.summary: Philadelphia-based company PECO, a subsidiary of Exelon (Nasdaq: EXC), has revealed that its President and CEO, David Velazquez, will be retiring. Velazquez, a seasoned Exelon employee with over 40 years of experience in the energy sector, took on the role in February 2024. Hailing from Pennsylvania, Velazquez has been actively involved in the local service area, fostering connections with businesses and community groups. Under his leadership, PECO has reinforced its dedication to the region.
news.headline: PECO's President and CEO, David Velazquez, retires after more than 40 years of service in the energy industry.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that over 3 billion people across the globe are at risk of contracting dengue fever. The disease, caused by a virus transmitted through mosquito bites, affects around 390 million individuals annually, leading to approximately 20,000 deaths. Scientists warn that climate change may exacerbate the spread of the disease, as warmer temperatures allow mosquitoes to thrive in new regions. With no specific treatment available, prevention efforts such as mosquito control and public awareness campaigns are crucial in combating this global health threat.
news.headline: Alibaba's growth disappoints, signaling challenges for Chinese tech industry.

news.summary: An analyst warned that if Powell does not indicate a need to quickly implement policy changes, markets may react defensively.
news.headline: Bitcoin prices drop due to weaker US wholesale prices; analyst warns of defensive market if Powell doesn't signal policy ease.

news.summary: Ideal Power Inc., a provider of innovative semiconductor power switch technology, reported its first quarter results for 2025. They completed SSCB prototypes three months ahead of schedule and secured an order from a Forbes Global 500 power management market leader for DC microgrid circuit protection. They also announced potential new EV projects with Stellantis and secured orders for B-TRAN devices from various customers. Despite higher R&D spending, operating expenses were $2.8 million with a net loss of $2.7 million in Q1 2025. Cash used in operations was $2.1 million, with cash and equivalents at $13.7 million. Revenue was $12,003 in Q1 2025. The company has set milestones for 2025, including securing further developments with Stellantis and increasing product power ratings. Their B-TRAN technology has 94 issued patents and a global presence. Interested parties can join the conference call on May 15 to discuss these results. The company focuses on eco-friendly energy solutions for various applications. Investors should consider the risks and uncertainties outlined in their reports. For more information about Ideal Power Inc., visit their website.
news.headline: Ideal Power reported Q1 2025 results, with $2.7 million net loss, $12,003 commercial revenue, $2.8 million operating expenses, and $13.7 million cash. Key milestones include securing next phase of development with Stellantis and increasing power rating of products. Conference call scheduled for May 15, 2025. For more info, visit www.IdealPower.com.
